response:
THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CONCENTRIC CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², WHERE R IS THE RADIUS. THE RADIUS OF THE FIRST CIRCLE IS r, AND EACH SUCCESSIVE CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE. THIS MEANS THAT THE RADIUS OF THE SECOND CIRCLE IS 1.1r, THE THIRD IS 1.21r, AND SO ON, UP TO THE EIGHTH CIRCLE. THE TOTAL AREA IS THEN THE SUM OF πR² FOR R FROM r TO 1.771561r (since 1.1^7 = 1.9487171, and 1.1^8 = 2.14358881, so 1.771561r is the radius of the 8th circle). THE TOTAL AREA IS πr²(1 + 1.1 + 1.21 + 1.331 + 1.4641 + 1.61051 + 1.771561). THIS IS THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES.

THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS DETERMINED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2π(1.4641r), AND THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS IS 0.5cm. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THEN THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY THE GAP. THIS GIVES THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THIS CIRCUMFERENCE. THIS IS THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THIS CIRCUMFERENCE.
